title: agalinis oligophylla
intro: Agalinis oligophylla, also known as False Foxglove, is a perennial plant species native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, as well as its ability to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes. The health benefits of Agalinis oligophylla include its potential to alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders, such as irritable bowel syndrome and Crohn's disease, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve overall digestive health. Agalinis oligophylla can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for medicinal use, with the tea being a popular method of consumption.
